blockers.description: IDOR / privilege escalation in backend/src/digital_resources/handlers.rs: issue_grant (line ~147 discards auth with `let _ = &auth`, line ~168 checks can_issue_resource_grant(&data.actor_org_id,...)) and issue_delegate (line ~257 checks can_issue_resource_grant(&data.granted_by_org_id,...)) authorize against a CLIENT-SUPPLIED org from the request body. Any authenticated user can name any ADMIN org and issue grants. Violates RSRC-BE-04 must-have ('actor derived from AuthGuard ... never persists on the client's say-so'). Flagged by background commit security review on 8ea8948.
blockers.workaround: Fix forward: derive authorizing org server-side from auth.claims.sub.

blockers.description: UNRESOLVED DECISION (user chose 'advise me'): JWT Claims = {sub, exp, iat, role} — no org field. The resolver authorizes by org (can_issue_resource_grant takes an org_id, checks ADMIN org-link / delegate). Must decide how to map authenticated caller -> authorizing org. Option A (org-based, matches resolver): query caller's org membership from auth.claims.sub via org_links/relations. Option B (role-based): authorize off JWT role (e.g. 'admin'), diverges from resolver. NEEDS INVESTIGATION: do seed users (admin/manager/operator/viewer) actually have org-link rows mapping them to orgs? If not, org-based check may not resolve for seed users — that gap is likely why the executor took the insecure shortcut.
blockers.workaround: Investigate person->org linkage in the live DB before choosing.

human_actions_pending.action: Decide authz model (Option A org-based vs Option B role-based) after the advise/investigation step
human_actions_pending.context: Gates the IDOR fix; user requested advice before deciding

decisions.decision: Paused 11-03 mid-execution rather than fix-forward or roll back
decisions.rationale: User chose to inspect handlers.rs and the finding personally; authz-model ambiguity needs resolution first (Rule 1 stop)
User paused for inspection; preserving state for review

next_action: Investigate person->org linkage in the live dev DB (psql :15530) — do seed users have org_links rows? Then advise on Option A vs B, get the user's decision, then fix the IDOR in handlers.rs.
